description_long: This mud patch features a rugged, uneven surface with a variety of shallow and deep craters, resembling the aftermath of heavy rainfall or repeated impact. The texture is predominantly rough, with some areas appearing more compacted and others more granular, suggesting a mix of wet and drying mud. The coloration is a blend of dark and light grays, with subtle variations indicating different moisture levels or soil compositions. The edges of the patch are irregular and blend gradually into the surrounding ground, lacking any sharp or artificial boundaries. No visible debris, footprints, or tire marks are present, and there are no signs of standing water or puddles. The overall appearance suggests a natural mud patch, possibly found in an open, unpaved area within an urban environment.
description: A rough, cratered mud patch with irregular edges and varying shades of gray, showing no visible debris or water accumulation.
description_view_0: From this angle, the mud patch displays a mostly flat surface with several shallow craters and a slightly raised edge on the left.
description_view_1: This view highlights a prominent crater near the top right and a more textured, granular area in the lower right quadrant.
description_view_2: Here, the largest crater is clearly visible in the lower right, with a mix of compacted and rougher textures across the surface.
description_view_3: This perspective shows multiple craters of varying sizes and a generally uneven, rugged surface with subtle elevation changes.
attribute_location: unpaved roadside or construction site within an urban area
attribute_boundary_definition: The boundaries are irregular and blend gradually into the surrounding ground, with no sharp transitions.
